question: A paper cites a pixel-labeling study built around a compact sample-wise prediction unit with well under a thousand learnable parameters, which invokes a foundational turn-of-the-millennium text on learning-theoretic bounds to justify smoothness constraints on its output space. In the citing paper, transitioning the architecture from its primary per-pixel objective to a whole-image recognition warmup at smaller input dimensions quadruples the number of tokens entering its fully-global interaction stage. A controlled comparison on the citing work's most compact variant further shows that replacing all spatial token-mixing operations with purely channel-wise modules at matched cost degrades the benchmark score by a modest but clear margin. Name the citing paper.
answer: TopFormer: Token Pyramid Transformer for Mobile Semantic Segmentation

question: A paper cites a technique that sidesteps divergence objectives entirely, instead deriving a bounded, fixed training signal from the disagreement among randomly initialized but never-updated models evaluated on expert data. That cited work validated on a vehicular scenario whose observation combined roughly twenty proximity inputs with a few motion-related features, and reported that a moment-alignment baseline collapsed to near-zero performance on a many-jointed locomotion creature. In the citing paper, a toy navigation task deliberately plants two inferior goal regions whose individual payoff is a small fraction of the true target's, and notes that this trap reliably diverts a standard off-policy learner toward suboptimal convergence. Identify the citing paper.
answer: f-IRL: Inverse Reinforcement Learning via State Marginal Matching

question: A paper that cites a blind image quality metric relying on fewer than half a dozen hand-crafted features — one gauging resemblance to a globally contrast-adjusted copy and the rest drawn from information-theoretic statistics, reported as the quickest among all competing no-reference methods in its original evaluation — uses that metric alongside others to compare the perceptual fidelity of several training collections. Separately, in a downstream depth-estimation experiment on a large-scale rendered multi-view benchmark, a footnote specifies that only one frame per scene was selected, and that frame sits at a non-initial position in the directory listing rather than the first available file. Identify this paper.
answer: Symmetric Parallax Attention for Stereo Image Super-Resolution

question: A paper's related work discussion references a study presented at a top venue around 2021 concerning computational sabotage of adaptive early-termination networks, wherein the authors' dataset-agnostic perturbation scheme funneled roughly one-sixth of affected inputs toward a single category on a small canonical classification benchmark, and which reserved a separate hundred-class dataset purely for cross-architecture generalization tests. In the citing paper itself, one traditional signal-degradation countermeasure tested as a baseline paradoxically reduced the model's primary hardware-independent loop metric by a small single-digit percentage on the first architecture examined, and the full output vocabulary is collapsed into a binary decision boundary within the training objective. Identify this citing paper.
answer: NICGSlowDown: Evaluating the Efficiency Robustness of Neural Image Caption Generation Models

question: A paper references a lightweight detection architecture tailored to resource-constrained hardware, whose distinctive front-end feature block was validated on a curated partition of a major recognition benchmark spanning roughly a hundred-plus fine-grained animal varieties. The citing work adopts that block to supplant an oversized early convolution that profiling revealed was responsible for a disproportionate fraction of the overall wall-clock cost. Among its own contributions, the citing work introduces a tensor arrangement whose name is formed by concatenating several single-letter axis identifiers, allowing a substantial majority of register contents to persist across neighboring spatial positions during convolution. Identify the citing paper.
answer: daBNN: A Super Fast Inference Framework for Binary Neural Networks on ARM devices

question: A paper cites a predecessor that repurposed a renowned sample-by-sample generative architecture from the audio domain to sequentially trace visual curves, relying on stochastic corruption of reference annotations during learning to counteract cumulative localization drift. In the citing work's own ablation, replacing a uniform binary region target on one output head with a peaked response concentrated on structure midlines yields more than two F1 points of improvement. A separate zero-shot cross-dataset experiment shows a well-known competitor—whose architecture propagates features sequentially across adjacent spatial slices—collapsing to near-zero accuracy. Identify this citing paper.
answer: Focus on Local: Detecting Lane Marker from Bottom Up via Key Point

question: A paper re-curates a publicly available radiograph collection by retaining only one anatomical projection orientation, excluding minors, and partitioning the remaining subjects by a binary demographic attribute before running its experiments. That same work cites a study in which pooling several copies of a bijective generative model—each launched from a different random initialization—still failed to prevent featureless uniform-value arrays from achieving a lossless-coding metric that exceeded the figure recorded for every standard natural evaluation set the study considered. Identify this paper.
answer: Natural Synthetic Anomalies for Self-Supervised Anomaly Detection and Localization

question: A paper whose authors, when justifying the axis range of a certain plot, note in a footnote that restricting a well-known single-channel handwriting benchmark to just two label categories yields a training partition slightly above twelve thousand examples. In a separate acknowledgment, a colleague is credited for pointing out a finding from an empirical catalog of milestone systems whose lead author was based at a university in the British Isles. That catalog separated entries into tiers via a statistical deviation threshold and reported that the lower tier's resource consumption doubled roughly every twenty months while the upper tier doubled in under a year. Identify this paper.
answer: A Solvable Model of Neural Scaling Laws

question: A paper cites a study that amassed roughly a thousand scripted transcriptions from a state-funded broadcaster and converted them into a large volume of weakly annotated utterances for a sequential labeling problem in a non-spoken modality. The citing paper references that study only to motivate stripping orthographic symbols from a supplementary bilingual corpus derived from recorded academic addresses. Elsewhere in the citing paper, a pair of sessions were dropped from a large multi-individual collection after their temporal capture frequency turned out to be half the nominal specification. Identify the citing paper.
answer: SLTUNET: A Simple Unified Model for Sign Language Translation

question: A paper borrows its cross-task degradation formula from a class-incremental visual recognition study presented at a premier computer-vision venue around 2020. That referenced study selects a hundred-category subset from a large-scale image benchmark using a hardcoded pseudorandom constant of 1993, and demonstrates that its optimized stored exemplars consistently migrate toward decision boundaries between categories in feature space. In the citing paper itself, a footnote documenting a cascading failure phenomenon shows a sentiment-oriented score on a zero-to-one scale falling from roughly the mid-sixties to the mid-forties after three successive rounds of unsupervised exposure to unrelated textual domains. Separately, a replay-oriented baseline in this same work is reported to require an episodic budget on the order of sixteen thousand tokens per previously encountered domain, described as the largest feasible allocation. Identify this citing paper.
answer: Continual Training of Language Models for Few-Shot Learning
